documentation.description: Jira Importer Toolkit Configuration File - Complete Reference
quick_start: 1. Copy this file to your project directory
2. Update jira.connection.site_address with your Jira URL (e.g., https://yourcompany.atlassian.net)
3. Set jira.connection.auth.email to your Atlassian account email
4. Set jira.connection.auth.api_token to your API token (get it from: https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens)
5. Set jira.project.key to your Jira project key (e.g., 'PROJ')
6. Update jira.issuetypes and jira.priorities to match your Jira instance

jira_connection.auth_api_token: Your API token from Atlassian account settings. You can also use environment variable JIRA_API_TOKEN
jira_connection.auth_mode: Set to 'basic' for email + API token authentication (currently the only supported method)

jira_project.key: Your Jira project key (e.g., 'PROJ', 'TEST'). Find it in your project URL or project settings

jira_issuetypes.description: List of issue types in your Jira with their hierarchy levels
jira_issuetypes.format: Array of objects with 'name' and 'level' properties
jira_issuetypes.example: Level 1 = highest (e.g., Initiative), Level 4 = lowest (e.g., Sub-Task)
jira_issuetypes.important: Names must match your Jira instance exactly (case-sensitive)

jira_priorities.description: List of valid priority values in your Jira
jira_priorities.important: Must match your Jira instance exactly (case-sensitive). Common values: Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest

row_skipping.skip_rowtype: If true, rows with RowType='SKIP' will be ignored during processing
row_skipping.skip_issuetypes: Array of issue type names to automatically skip (e.g., ['comment', 'note', 'skip'])

auto_fix.fixers_disabled: Array of problem codes to disable auto-fixing for. Example: ['project_key.mismatch', 'priority.missing']
auto_fix.note: Only applies when --auto-fix flag is used. Leave empty array [] to enable all fixers

estimates.multiply_by_60: Set to true if you're using Jira Cloud and estimates need to be multiplied by 60 (legacy compatibility)
estimates.accept_integers_as: How to interpret plain integer estimates: 'seconds' or 'minutes' (default: 'seconds')
estimates.fields: Which columns to validate as estimates: ['estimate', 'origest'] or just one

custom_fields.description: Configure custom Jira fields you want to import
custom_fields.format: Array of objects with 'name' (Excel column name), 'id' (Jira field ID like customfield_10125), and 'type' (text, number, date, select, or any)

application_behavior.auto_open_page: If true, automatically opens Jira page after CSV export (CSV mode only)
application_behavior.delete_enabled: If true, automatically deletes temporary artifacts after processing
application_behavior.write_processing_meta: If true, writes processing metadata back to Excel file
application_behavior.write_report_table: If true, writes validation report table back to Excel file

logging.console_output: If true, shows log messages in console
logging.log_level: Logging level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL
logging.max_log_files: Maximum number of log files to keep (oldest files are deleted)
logging.max_log_size_mb: Maximum size of each log file in megabytes

environment_variables.description: You can use environment variables instead of hardcoding sensitive values
environment_variables.JIRA_EMAIL: Your Atlassian account email (alternative to jira.connection.auth.email)

common_examples.skip_rows: To skip rows with RowType='SKIP' and issue types 'comment' and 'note': Set skip_rowtype: true and skip_issuetypes: ['comment', 'note']
common_examples.disable_specific_fixes: To prevent auto-fixing priority issues: Set fixers.disabled: ['priority.missing']
common_examples.jira_cloud_estimates: If estimates are wrong in Jira Cloud: Set jira.cloud.estimate.multiply_by_60: true
common_examples.custom_priorities: To use different priority names: Update jira.priorities array to match your Jira instance exactly

important_notes: This documentation section is for reference only - it is not read by the application
All validation rules are under 'validation.*' section
All Jira settings (issue types, priorities, custom fields) are under 'jira.*' section
Issue type and priority names are case-sensitive and must match your Jira instance exactly
You can store credentials securely using: jira-importer.exe --credentials run
For Excel-based configuration, use the Config sheet in your Excel file and run with -ce flag

section_descriptions.app: Application behavior settings (logging, artifacts, import options)
section_descriptions.jira: Jira connection, project, and validation settings (issue types, priorities, custom fields)
section_descriptions.metadata: Configuration file metadata (version, last updated date)
